parameters.query:
SELECT tc.*,
  pi.access_token AS bot_token,
  (SELECT json_agg(json_build_object('key', m.key, 'value', m.value))
   FROM table_2 m
   WHERE m.business_id = tc.business_id
     AND m.is_active = true
     AND (m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > now())
  ) as agent_memories
FROM table_3 tc
JOIN table_4 pi
  ON pi.business_id = tc.business_id AND pi.platform = 'telegram'
WHERE tc.tenant_id   = $1::uuid
  AND tc.business_id = $2::uuid

parameters.toolDescription:
=Retrieve internal product knowledge about MyGuy and MySquad.

WHEN TO USE:
- "What can you do?"
- "How does MyGuy work?"
- "Tell me about stored commands"
- "What other agents are there?"
- "What is MySquad?"
- product capability or internal platform questions

DO NOT USE FOR:
- general world knowledge
- finance actions
- Gmail or Calendar operations
- customer orders or dispatch lookups

RULES:
- Answer from table_14 documentation.
- Be honest if the documentation does not cover it.

parameters.description:
=Save a new product image or replace an existing one.

WHEN TO USE:
- Owner sends a single photo and context makes clear it is a product (caption has product name, or prior conversation was about catalogue)
- "save this as [product]"
- "replace my [product] image"
- "this is my [product]"

REQUIRED:
- product_name
- image_url (use Source image URL from table_15 context)

RULES:
- Only use when the current owner message includes an image and the product name is known.
- If product name is missing, ask once before calling.
- Do not invent image URLs.

value.price: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('price', 'Numeric price if mentioned in caption or conversation. Pass null if not mentioned.', 'number') || null }}

parameters.description:
=Generate a Catalogue Manager link for the owner to upload and manage product photos in bulk.

WHEN TO USE:
- Owner says 'add more products', 'upload more photos', 'manage my catalogue', 'add product images', 'I want to add products'
- Any request to add or manage multiple product photos
- Owner wants to bulk upload

Do NOT use for saving a single photo already sent in chat (use Save Product Image Tool instead).

Returns: { url: "[URL]" } — use this url in your response after the tool completes.

parameters.toolDescription:
=Permanently delete a saved product image.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Delete my [product] image"
- "Remove [product] photo"
- "I don't need this product image anymore"

RULES:
- Only use after the target product image is clear.
- If unclear, call List Product Images Tool first.
- Confirm once before deleting.
parameters.query:
DELETE FROM table_16
column_1 $1::uuid
  AND (product_name ILIKE $2 OR display_name ILIKE $2)
RETURNING product_name, display_name

parameters.toolDescription:
=List saved product image names for this business.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Show my product images"
- "What photos do I have?"
- "List my uploads"
- Before delete or replace when owner is unsure of the exact name
parameters.query:
SELECT COALESCE(display_name, product_name) AS name, price, show_on_store, created_at
FROM table_16
column_2 $1::uuid
  column_1 $2::uuid
  column_3 'product'
ORDER BY COALESCE(display_name, product_name) ASC

parameters.description:
=Send saved product images to the owner as Telegram buttons.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Send me my product images"
- "Let me see my uploads"
- "Show me my saved catalogue photos"

value.filter: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('filter', 'Optional product keyword to filter. Empty string shows all.', 'string') || '' }}

parameters.toolDescription:
=Save a stable business fact or operating rule.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Remember that our delivery fee is ₦500"
- "Always note that we don't do refunds"
- Stable business policies or reference facts
parameters.query:
INSERT INTO table_2 (tenant_id, business_id, key, value, source)
VALUES ($1::uuid, $2::uuid, $3, $4, 'owner_chat')
ON CONFLICT (business_id, key)
DO UPDATE column_4 $4, updated_at = now(), is_active = true
RETURNING key, value

parameters.toolDescription:
=Deactivate a saved business memory.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Forget that delivery fee rule"
- "Remove that refund rule"
parameters.query:
UPDATE table_2
column_5 false, updated_at = now()
column_1 $1::uuid column_6 $2
RETURNING key

parameters.toolDescription:
=Update table_9, location, or description.

WHEN TO USE:
- "Update table_1 catalogue"
- "Change my location"
- "Update table_1 business description"

Only populate fields the owner explicitly wants changed.
parameters.query:
UPDATE table_17
column_7 CASE WHEN NULLIF($2, '') IS NOT NULL THEN $2 ELSE catalogue_summary END,
  location          = CASE WHEN NULLIF($3, '') IS NOT NULL THEN $3 ELSE location END,
  description       = CASE WHEN NULLIF($4, '') IS NOT NULL THEN $4 ELSE description END,
  updated_at        = now()
column_8 $1::uuid
RETURNING business_name, catalogue_summary, location, description

parameters.description: =Update table_5 agent's name, tone, or default reply language.

value.agent_name: ={{ $fromAI('agent_name', 'New name. Leave blank to keep current.', 'string') || '' }}
value.tone_summary: ={{ $fromAI('tone_summary', 'New tone description. Leave blank to keep current.', 'string') || '' }}
value.reply_language: ={{ $fromAI('reply_language', 'en or pidgin. Leave blank to keep current.', 'string') || '' }}

parameters.description: =Read-only lookup for saved product images and dispatch records.

parameters.toolDescription: =Get business performance data — orders, revenue, customers, top products, cancellations. Use when owner asks how their business is doing.
parameters.query:
WITH period_bounds AS (
  SELECT
    CASE $3
      WHEN 'today'      THEN now() - interval '24 hours'
      WHEN 'yesterday'  THEN now() - interval '48 hours'
      WHEN 'this_week'  THEN now() - interval '7 days'
      WHEN 'this_month' THEN now() - interval '30 days'
      WHEN 'last_month' THEN now() - interval '60 days'
      ELSE                   now() - interval '7 days'
    END AS start_at,
    CASE $3
      WHEN 'yesterday'  THEN now() - interval '24 hours'
      WHEN 'last_month' THEN now() - interval '30 days'
      ELSE                   now()
    END AS end_at
),
order_stats AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(*)                                      AS total_orders,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (column_9 'cancelled')  AS cancelled_orders,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'delivered')) AS pending_orders,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE payment_status IN ('unpaid','proof_submitted') AND status NOT IN ('cancelled','delivered')) AS pending_payments,
    COALESCE(SUM(total_amount) FILTER (WHERE status NOT IN ('cancelled')), 0) AS total_revenue,
    COALESCE(AVG(total_amount) FILTER (WHERE status NOT IN ('cancelled')), 0) AS avg_order_value,
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_chat_id) AS ordering_customers
  FROM table_18, period_bounds
  column_1 $1::uuid column_10= start_at column_10 end_at
),
customer_stats AS (
  SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT platform_id) AS total_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT platform_id) FILTER (column_10= (SELECT start_at FROM table_19)) AS new_customers
  FROM table_20
  column_1 $1::uuid
    column_11= (SELECT start_at FROM table_19)
    column_11  (SELECT end_at   FROM table_19)
),
top_products AS (
  SELECT item->>'name' AS product_name,
    SUM((item->>'quantity')::int) AS qty_sold,
    SUM((item->>'unit_price')::numeric * (item->>'quantity')::int) AS product_revenue
  FROM table_18, jsonb_array_elements(items) AS item, period_bounds
  column_1 $1::uuid column_10= start_at column_10 end_at AND status NOT IN ('cancelled')
  GROUP BY product_name ORDER BY qty_sold DESC LIMIT 5
),
escalation_stats AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_escalations,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (column_9 'answered') AS answered,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (column_9 'pending')  AS unanswered
  FROM table_21
  column_1 $1::uuid
    column_10= (SELECT start_at FROM table_19)
    column_10  (SELECT end_at   FROM table_19)
)
SELECT $3 AS period,
  (SELECT total_orders       FROM table_22)             AS total_orders,
  (SELECT cancelled_orders   FROM table_22)             AS cancelled_orders,
  (SELECT pending_orders     FROM table_22)             AS pending_orders,
  (SELECT pending_payments   FROM table_22)             AS pending_payments,
  (SELECT total_revenue      FROM table_22)             AS total_revenue,
  (SELECT avg_order_value    FROM table_22)             AS avg_order_value,
  (SELECT ordering_customers FROM table_22)             AS ordering_customers,
  (SELECT total_customers    FROM table_23)          AS active_customers,
  (SELECT new_customers      FROM table_23)          AS new_customers,
  (SELECT json_agg(row_to_json(tp)) FROM table_24 tp)  AS top_products,
  (SELECT total_escalations  FROM table_25)        AS total_escalations,
  (SELECT unanswered         FROM table_25)        AS unanswered_escalations

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [$('Incoming Payload').first().json.business_id, $('Incoming Payload').first().json.tenant_id, $fromAI('period', 'today, yesterday, this_week, this_month, last_month', 'string') || 'this_week'] }}

parameters.jsCode:
const data = $input.first().json;
const incoming = $('Incoming Payload').first().json;
return { json: {
  output:          data.output,
  response_type:   data.response_type,
  message_type_in: data.message_type_in || 'text',
  gen_prompt:      data.gen_prompt      || null,
  gen_mode:        data.gen_mode        || null,
  buffer_message:  data.buffer_message  || null,
  caption:         data.caption         || null,
  bot_token:                  $('Build Agent B Config').first().json.bot_token,
  chat_id:                    incoming.chat_id,
  conversation_id:            incoming.conversation_id,
  tenant_id:                  incoming.tenant_id,
  user_message:               incoming.text || incoming.caption || '',
  is_owner:                   true,
  has_new_order:              data.has_new_order              || false,
  order_number:               data.order_number               || null,
  order_summary:              data.order_summary              || null,
  has_order_confirmed:        data.has_order_confirmed        || false,
  confirmed_order_number:     data.confirmed_order_number     || null,
  confirmed_customer_chat_id: data.confirmed_customer_chat_id || null,
  payment_confirmed:          data.payment_confirmed          || false,
  payment_order_number:       data.payment_order_number       || null,
  broadcast_message:          data.broadcast_message          || null,
}};

parameters.toolDescription:
=Fetch the public store link for this business — the page customers see when browsing products.

USE WHEN:
- "what link do I share with customers?"
- "send me my shop link" / "my store page" / "my store URL"
- "I want to send my catalogue to a customer"
- Owner wants the public-facing product page URL

DO NOT USE:
- When owner wants to upload or manage products (use Generate Catalogue Link Tool)

Returns: { store_url: "[URL]" } — include this url in your response.
parameters.query:
SELECT '[URL]' || store_token AS store_url
FROM table_17
column_8 $1::uuid
LIMIT 1
